title: A global workspace in language models
excerpt: Interpretability research reporting an emergent mental workspace in Claude that holds internal thoughts which do not appear in the model output.
filter_reason: Anthropic interpretability finding of an emergent internal workspace in Claude - capability-relevant research on how frontier models actually compute.
summary: Anthropic interpretability researchers report evidence of an emergent 'global workspace' in Claude: an internal mental scratch space holding thoughts that never surface in the model's text output. If it holds up, this reshapes assumptions that chain-of-thought text reflects what the model is doing internally, with direct implications for monitoring, faithfulness of reasoning traces, and how much latent computation frontier models carry out unseen.
